name: Firebase
description: Monitor Firebase Authentication MFA settings, user access, and security rules for Firestore and Cloud Storage

config.setupInstructions:
1. Go to Google Cloud Console > APIs & Credentials for your Firebase project
2. Enable these two APIs:
   - Identity Toolkit API: https://console.developers.google.com/apis/api/identitytoolkit.googleapis.com/overview
   - Firebase Rules API: https://console.developers.google.com/apis/api/firebaserules.googleapis.com/overview
3. Make sure both APIs are enabled on the SAME project where your Firebase app lives
4. Use your existing Google OAuth app or create one with scopes: identitytoolkit and firebase.readonly
5. Enter your Firebase Project ID when connecting (found in Firebase Console > Project Settings)

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
IMPORTANT вЂ” Account permissions

The Google account you sign in with must have at least the "Firebase Viewer" role (roles/firebase.viewer) on the Firebase project you want to monitor. Owners and Editors already have it by default.

If your account does not, ask a project admin to grant it:
1. Open https://console.developers.google.com/iam-admin/iam?project=YOUR_PROJECT_ID
2. Click "+ Grant Access" (or pencil-edit your existing entry)
3. Add role: "Firebase Viewer"
4. Save and wait ~1 minute for propagation

Then come back here and click Connect. If a check fails with "The caller does not have permission", this is almost always the fix.
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

checks.name: Employee Access
checks.description: Reviews Firebase Authentication users, their sign-in providers, MFA enrollment, and last activity

checks.name: Security Rules
checks.description: Checks that Firestore and Cloud Storage security rules are deployed and not using wide-open defaults
